description: Browser-based frontend for the Crypto Bet Engine. Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, dark trading theme, real-time WebSocket prices, bet placement with odds/exposure info, bet history. Served as static files from Spring Boot.

userStories.title: Add nickname field to wallet backend
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need the wallet API to accept and return a nickname so users can identify their wallets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Flyway migration V5: ALTER TABLE wallets ADD COLUMN nickname VARCHAR(50)
Add nickname field to Wallet entity (nullable String)
Update CreateWalletRequest to accept optional nickname field
Update WalletResponse to include nickname
POST /api/wallets with {"initialBalance": 100, "nickname": "Player1"} stores and returns nickname
Existing wallets without nickname return null for nickname field
Existing tests still pass
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: Backend change needed before frontend can use nicknames. Keep nickname optional for backwards compat.

userStories.title: Enable SockJS fallback on WebSocket endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need SockJS support so the browser can connect reliably to STOMP.
acceptanceCriteria: Update WebSocketConfig: add .withSockJS() to the /ws endpoint registration
Add sockjs dependency if needed (spring-boot-starter-websocket should include it)
Existing WebSocket tests still pass
./gradlew test passes
userStories.notes: Browsers need SockJS fallback for reliable STOMP. Quick config change.

userStories.title: App shell — HTML structure and dark theme CSS
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished dark-themed layout so the app feels like a real trading platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/main/resources/static/index.html with semantic HTML structure
Create src/main/resources/static/css/style.css with dark theme
Dark palette: #0f0f1a (body bg), #1a1a2e (cards), #16213e (accents), #e0e0e0 (text)
Green #00e676 for UP/WON, Red #ff1744 for DOWN/LOST, Yellow #ffd600 for PUSH
CSS custom properties for all theme colors
Header bar with app name 'CryptoBet' and wallet indicator placeholder
Navigation: Dashboard and Bet History tabs/links
Main content area with placeholder sections: price ticker, bet panel, active bets
Responsive: sidebar + main on desktop (>768px), stacked on mobile
Google Fonts: JetBrains Mono for numbers, system font stack for UI text
Loading index.html in browser shows styled dark layout
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Static files only. No JS yet. Create the visual foundation.

userStories.title: JavaScript foundation — API client and app initialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the JS structure with fetch wrapper and app state management.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/main/resources/static/js/app.js
App state object: { walletId, nickname, balance, prices: {}, bets: [] }
API helper: api.get(path), api.post(path, body) wrapping fetch() with error handling
API helper returns parsed JSON on success, throws with error message on failure
Toast notification system: showToast(message, type) where type is success/error/warning
Toast appears top-right, auto-dismisses after 5 seconds, stackable
CSS for toast notifications in style.css
View router: showView(name) toggles visibility of dashboard vs history sections
Navigation links switch views without page reload
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Foundation JS — no API calls yet, just structure.

userStories.title: Wallet creation modal
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to create a wallet with a nickname so I can start using the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: On page load, check localStorage for walletId
If no walletId, show modal overlay with create-wallet form
Form fields: nickname (text, required, min 2 chars), initial balance (number, required, > 0)
Submit calls POST /api/wallets with { nickname, initialBalance }
On success: store walletId and nickname in localStorage, close modal, initialize app
On error: show error message in modal
Modal cannot be dismissed without creating wallet
Client-side validation with inline error messages
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-F01 (nickname backend) and US-F04 (API client).

userStories.title: Wallet dashboard — balance and deposit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my wallet balance and deposit funds.
acceptanceCriteria: On app init (after wallet exists), fetch GET /api/wallets/{id}
Display in header/sidebar: nickname, balance formatted as $X,XXX.XX, wallet ID (first 8 chars)
Deposit button opens inline form with amount input
Deposit calls POST /api/wallets/{id}/deposit with { amount }
Balance updates in UI after successful deposit, show success toast
Handle wallet-not-found: clear localStorage, show create-wallet modal
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: WebSocket connection and live price display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see real-time crypto prices updating live.
acceptanceCriteria: Load SockJS and STOMP.js from CDN (script tags in index.html)
Connect to STOMP over SockJS at /ws endpoint
Subscribe to /topic/prices for all price updates
Display price card for each symbol (BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT)
Each card shows: symbol name, current price formatted with commas, direction arrow
Price text flashes green on increase, red on decrease (CSS animation, 300ms)
Connection status indicator in header: green dot = connected, yellow = reconnecting, red = disconnected
Auto-reconnect on disconnect with 2s delay
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: First WebSocket story. Keep sparkline for next story to keep scope small.

userStories.title: Price sparkline charts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a mini chart of recent price history for each symbol.
acceptanceCriteria: Add canvas element to each price card
Store last 50 price points per symbol in memory array
Draw sparkline on canvas: line chart, no axes, just the price curve
Line color: green if current > first point, red if lower, gray if equal
Canvas redraws on each new price update
Canvas size: ~200x60px on desktop, scales on mobile
Sparkline draws smoothly without flicker
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Canvas-based, no libraries. Keep it simple.

userStories.title: Bet placement form with odds and exposure
userStories.description: As a user, I want to place a bet and see odds, exposure, and estimated payout before confirming.
acceptanceCriteria: Bet panel with: symbol dropdown (BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT), direction toggle (UP/DOWN buttons), amount input
UP button styled green, DOWN button styled red, selected state highlighted
On symbol + direction selection, fetch GET /api/odds?symbol={s}&direction={d}
On symbol selection, fetch GET /api/exposure/{symbol}
Display: current odds, symbol exposure, estimated payout (amount * odds)
Estimated payout updates live as amount changes
Amount validation: min 1, cannot exceed wallet balance
Submit button disabled until all fields valid
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Form and info display only. Confirmation and API call in next story.

userStories.title: Bet placement confirmation and submission
userStories.description: As a user, I want a confirmation step before my bet is placed.
acceptanceCriteria: Click 'Place Bet' shows confirmation overlay/section with bet summary
Summary shows: symbol, direction, amount, odds, estimated payout
Confirm button calls POST /api/bets with { walletId, symbol, direction, amount }
On success: show success toast with bet ID, refresh wallet balance, clear form
On error: show error toast (insufficient balance, exposure limit, etc.)
Buttons disabled during API call to prevent double-submit
Cancel returns to form without changes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Active bets display on dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my currently pending bets on the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Section below bet panel showing active/pending bets
Fetch from GET /api/wallets/{id}/bets?status=PENDING&page=0&size=10
Each bet shows: symbol, direction (colored), amount, placed time (relative), countdown to resolution
Countdown updates every second showing time remaining
Refresh list after placing a new bet
Empty state message: 'No active bets'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Live bet settlement notifications
userStories.description: As a user, I want real-time notifications when my bets settle.
acceptanceCriteria: Subscribe to /topic/bets/{walletId} via STOMP (reuse existing connection)
On bet_update event, show toast: green for WON (with payout), red for LOST, yellow for PUSH/VOID
Auto-refresh wallet balance after receiving settlement
Remove settled bet from active bets list
If on history view, refresh history data
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Bet history view with filters and pagination
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse my past bets to review my performance.
acceptanceCriteria: History view accessible via navigation tab
Fetch from GET /api/wallets/{id}/bets?page=0&size=20
Filter tabs: All, Won, Lost, Push, Void — each calls API with status param
Table/list: symbol, direction, amount, odds, payout, status badge, placed time, settled time
Status badges color-coded: green=WON, red=LOST, yellow=PUSH, gray=VOID
Pagination: Previous/Next buttons, 'Page X of Y' indicator
Active tab highlighted, filter persists when paginating
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wallet switching
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between wallets or create a new one.
acceptanceCriteria: Wallet section in header is clickable, opens dropdown menu
Dropdown shows: current nickname, wallet ID (truncated)
Option: 'Switch Wallet' — shows input for wallet ID, validates via GET /api/wallets/{id}
Option: 'Create New Wallet' — opens the create-wallet modal
On switch: update localStorage, reinitialize app (reload all data, resubscribe WebSocket)
Invalid wallet ID shows error in dropdown
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Loading states and error handling polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback when things are loading or go wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading spinner shown during initial wallet fetch
Skeleton placeholder cards for price ticker while WebSocket connects
All buttons show loading state (spinner/disabled) during API calls
Network failure: banner at top 'Connection lost — retrying...' with retry countdown
API errors show user-friendly messages (not raw JSON)
Wallet not found on load: clear state, show create modal with explanation
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Polish pass. All core features should work before this story.
